name: Microsoft 365
description: Microsoft's productivity suite (Exchange Online, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams) with compliance evidence from the Microsoft Purview unified audit log, DLP and retention policies, sharing reports and Secure Score. Identity and sign-in evidence is covered by the Microsoft Entra ID template.

steps.action: Sign in to the Microsoft Purview portal (purview.microsoft.com), select the Audit solution card and open the Search page. Set the date and time range (UTC) to cover the collection period with no gap since the previous export
steps.permissions_required: Audit Logs or View-Only Audit Logs role (the Audit Reader role group grants View-Only Audit Logs)
steps.security_note: Use a dedicated account in the Audit Reader role group for evidence collection, not a Global Administrator login
steps.audit_note: Unified audit log records are generated by Microsoft, timestamped in UTC and cannot be edited by tenant administrators. Audit (Standard) retains records for 180 days; E5/Audit (Premium) retains Exchange, SharePoint and Entra records for one year

steps.action: Scope the search to the Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive and Teams workloads (or specific record types such as SharePointSharingOperation for external sharing evidence), give the search job a date-stamped name and select Search. Search jobs run in the background and completed jobs are kept for 30 days
steps.permissions_required: Audit Logs or View-Only Audit Logs role
steps.audit_note: Audit records for core workloads are typically available 60-90 minutes after the event; search at least an hour after the period being evidenced ends

steps.action: Open the completed search job and select Export on the command bar to download the results as a CSV (up to 50,000 rows for Audit (Standard), 1,000,000 rows for Audit (Premium)). Save with a date-stamped filename
steps.audit_note: The AuditData column contains the full event detail as JSON; keep it intact in the exported file as it is the authoritative record

steps.action: In the Purview portal go to Solutions > Data loss prevention > Policies and capture the policy list showing each DLP policy's name, locations and status (On/Off) as a screenshot or export, to evidence that DLP is enforced across Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive and Teams
steps.permissions_required: Compliance Administrator or Compliance Data Administrator

steps.action: In the Purview portal go to Solutions > Data Lifecycle Management > Policies > Retention policies and capture the list of retention policies, their locations and distribution status to evidence records-retention controls
steps.permissions_required: Compliance Administrator (or a role group with data lifecycle management permissions)

steps.action: In the Microsoft Defender portal (security.microsoft.com) open the Microsoft Secure Score page, capture the current score, the history tab trend and the top improvement actions, then upload all exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with evidence ID references
steps.permissions_required: Security Reader (read-only view of Secure Score)
steps.security_note: Review exports before upload to confirm they contain no credentials or message content beyond what the evidence requires
steps.audit_note: Secure Score history is dated by Microsoft, so a screenshot of the history tab demonstrates trend over time, not just a point-in-time value

L2.title: Microsoft 365 Scheduled and Policy-Driven Evidence Delivery
L2.estimated_time: 1 hour initial setup, then 10 minutes per cycle

steps.action: Note that Microsoft Purview Audit does not offer natively scheduled recurring audit exports — search jobs are started on demand. Compensate by creating audit log retention policies (Purview portal > Audit > Policies) so records remain searchable for the full period between collections (up to one year, or 10 years with the add-on licence)
steps.permissions_required: Organization Configuration role in the Microsoft Purview portal (requires Audit (Premium)/E5 licensing)
steps.audit_note: A retention policy is itself evidence that audit data availability is managed; export the policy list as part of the evidence pack

steps.action: Run the built-in SharePoint sharing report on key sites (site Settings > Site usage > Shared with external users > Run report) and save the CSV output to a restricted evidence document library; an email link is delivered when the report completes. Repeat for OneDrive via OneDrive settings > More settings > Run sharing report
steps.permissions_required: Site admin on each site being reported
steps.security_note: Save reports to a folder or library that only the compliance team can access — the CSV lists every unique file, user, permission and sharing link on the site
steps.audit_note: The report captures guest access and sharing-link usage; run it on a fixed cadence so successive reports evidence change over time

steps.action: Configure alert policies (Purview portal > Policies > Alert policies) so that DLP rule matches and unusual external-sharing activity automatically email notifications to a dedicated, mailbox-retained evidence address
steps.security_note: Limit alert recipients to authorised compliance personnel; alert emails can contain sensitive incident detail
steps.audit_note: Alert emails are event-driven rather than scheduled, but the retained mailbox provides a continuous, timestamped record of policy enforcement between collection cycles

steps.action: Set a recurring monthly task to capture the Microsoft Secure Score history tab in the Defender portal; the history is retained by Microsoft so each capture evidences the trend since the last one
File the delivered sharing reports, alert emails and Secure Score captures into the evidence repository on the collection cadence, and verify each cycle that there are no gaps in the periods covered
steps.audit_note: Record the covered date range against each filed item so completeness can be demonstrated at audit

steps.action: Register an application in the Microsoft Entra admin centre (Identity > Applications > App registrations) and grant it the application permission ActivityFeed.Read on the Office 365 Management APIs, plus SecurityEvents.Read.All on Microsoft Graph for Secure Score. Grant tenant-wide admin consent
steps.permissions_required: Cloud Application Administrator to register the app; Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator to grant admin consent
steps.security_note: Authenticate with a certificate or a client secret held in a secrets manager (e.g. Azure Key Vault) — never in source control. Scope the app to the minimum read-only permissions listed
steps.audit_note: App registration and consent grants are themselves recorded in the Entra audit log, evidencing controlled provisioning

steps.action: Start Management Activity API subscriptions for each content type by calling POST https://manage.office.com/api/v1.0/{tenant_id}/activity/feed/subscriptions/start?contentType=Audit.Exchange (repeat for Audit.SharePoint, Audit.General and DLP.All)
steps.permissions_required: App token containing the ActivityFeed.Read claim
steps.audit_note: It can take up to 12 hours after a subscription starts for the first content blobs to become available — allow for this before relying on the feed

steps.action: Schedule a daily job that calls GET https://manage.office.com/api/v1.0/{tenant_id}/activity/feed/subscriptions/content?contentType={type} to list available content, downloads each content blob URI and writes the JSON events to the evidence store
steps.security_note: Store the client credential in a secrets manager and rotate it on your credential rotation schedule
steps.audit_note: Content blobs are only available for 7 days after they are created — collect at least daily so nothing ages out of the feed

steps.action: Extend the job to call GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/security/secureScores on Microsoft Graph and store the daily secure score snapshot alongside the audit data
Implement error handling and alerting: retry transient failures, alert the compliance team on any run that fails or returns HTTP errors, and log every run's outcome
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should trigger re-collection within the 7-day content availability window

steps.action: Validate collected data daily — compare event counts per workload against a rolling baseline and flag any drop that could indicate a silent collection gap
steps.audit_note: Volume validation per contentType (Exchange, SharePoint, General, DLP) provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: Microsoft 365 Managed Evidence Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: Enable the Microsoft 365 (formerly Office 365) data connector in Microsoft Sentinel to ingest the unified audit log for Exchange, SharePoint/OneDrive and Teams continuously into the OfficeActivity table of your Log Analytics workspace
steps.permissions_required: Global Administrator or Security Administrator on the tenant, plus Microsoft Sentinel Contributor on the workspace
steps.security_note: Restrict workspace read access to security and compliance personnel; the ingested audit data includes user activity detail
steps.audit_note: Sentinel ingestion timestamps every record on arrival, giving an independent, immutable copy of the audit trail outside the 180-day/1-year Purview retention window

steps.action: For near-real-time delivery outside Sentinel, register a webhook on your Management Activity API subscriptions (start subscription with a webhook property) so Microsoft notifies your collector as soon as new content blobs are available, instead of polling
steps.security_note: The webhook endpoint must be HTTPS and should validate the Webhook-ValidationCode challenge on subscription start; authenticate inbound notifications before fetching content

steps.action: Build collection completeness metrics: track events per day per workload in OfficeActivity, ingestion latency against the 60-90 minute typical audit availability, and coverage across all licensed workloads (Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams, DLP)
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are the key evidence that collection is a managed process rather than best-effort

steps.action: Create Sentinel scheduled analytics rules (KQL) that alert when a workload's event volume drops below baseline or ingestion stalls, and a workbook dashboard showing events/day, latency and failed collections
steps.audit_note: Alerting on collection gaps, and the record of those alerts, demonstrates active monitoring of evidence continuity

steps.action: Run an automated quarterly review that generates a collection health report — coverage by workload, gap incidents and their resolution, retention policy status and Secure Score trend — and file it as evidence of the managed pipeline
steps.audit_note: Pair the health report with the Purview audit retention policy export to evidence both continuous collection and guaranteed source retention
